name: Moncayo — gravel circular from Monasterio de Veruela
name_original: Moncayo — gravel circular desde el Monasterio de Veruela
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vera De Moncayo are characterized by the diverse landscapes at the foot of Moncayo Mountain, the highest point in the Iberian System. The region features significant elevation gains, transitioning from glacial remnants at higher altitudes to lush forests of holm oaks, Pyrenean oaks, and extensive beech forests on its slopes. This varied terrain, part of the Moncayo Natural Park, offers a dynamic environment for cyclists. Roads are generally in good condition, providing a suitable setting for challenging rides. Best touring cycling routes around Vera De Moncayo The most popular touring cycling route is Moncayo — gravel circular from Monasterio de Veruela, a 28.2 miles (45.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete, featuring over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Winding Road Through Forest – Fuente de los Frailes loop from Lituénigo, a difficult 27.2 miles (43.9 km) path that winds through forest with over 800 meters of ascent. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Vera de Moncayo, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail leading through the Moncayo foothills, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Vera De Moncayo is defined by the Moncayo mountain range, diverse forest types, and varied terrain with significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to very hard bike rides. The routes in Vera De Moncayo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vera De Moncayo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Vera De Moncayo?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes in the Vera De Moncayo area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on touring cycling routes in Vera De Moncayo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Vera De Moncayo are characterized by diverse landscapes at the foot of the majestic Moncayo Mountain. You can expect significant elevation gains, with routes transitioning from glacial remnants at higher altitudes to lush forests of holm oaks, Pyrenean oaks, and extensive beech forests on its slopes. The region offers options from moderate to very hard bike rides, with many routes featuring over 700 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Vera De Moncayo area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the region are designed as loops. For example, you can try the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993131894/moncayo-gravel-circular-from-monasterio-de-veruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo — gravel circular from Monasterio de Veruela&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.4 km with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, or the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24314487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Vera de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.8 km route through the Moncayo foothills.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge will find several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993131894/moncayo-gravel-circular-from-monasterio-de-veruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo — gravel circular from Monasterio de Veruela&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.4 km route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Añón de Moncayo – Mirador de La Diezma Viewpoint loop from Vera de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, a 53.0 km ride with 930 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easier touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes in Vera De Moncayo involve significant elevation, there are approximately 5 easier touring cycling routes available. These routes offer a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to enjoy the region's beauty.

Q: What natural features and landscapes will I encounter while cycling around Vera De Moncayo?
A: Cycling around Vera De Moncayo offers a journey through the diverse landscapes of the Moncayo Natural Park. You'll experience transitions from high-altitude glacial remnants to lush forests of holm oaks, Pyrenean oaks, and extensive beech forests. The region is also home to varied wildlife and offers captivating colors throughout its varied terrain.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You can find stunning views from places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Peñas de Herrera&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent natural landmark. Additionally, historical sites like the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Veruela are often accessible from cycling routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Vera De Moncayo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Vera De Moncayo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning natural beauty of the Moncayo Natural Park, and the generally good condition of the roads with minimal traffic, which enhances the cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vera De Moncayo?
A: The region experiences mild, short summers and long, cold winters. For touring cycling, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather conditions, avoiding the heat of summer and the cold of winter, making it ideal for enjoying the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Vera De Moncayo?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are easier routes available that could be suitable for families. It's recommended to check the difficulty grade and elevation profiles of specific routes to ensure they match your family's fitness level. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate'.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Vera De Moncayo?
A: Vera de Moncayo and nearby villages like Alcalá de Moncayo and Añón offer options for gastronomy, including authentic Aragonese cuisine. The region is also known for the Campo de Borja wine region. For accommodation, you can find places to stay in Vera de Moncayo and surrounding areas, catering to cyclists.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in the Moncayo Natural Park?
A: The region is part of the Moncayo Natural Park. While general cycling on established roads and trails typically does not require a specific permit, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any specific restrictions or protected areas within the park, especially if venturing off marked paths.
tips: We are in the monastery of Santa María de Veruela, a magical place in the province of Zaragoza. The monastery combines Romanesque, Gothic, Renaissance and Baroque architecture and is surrounded by a wall. We can also visit the cloister or the exhibition of the Bécquer brothers since they worked and stayed here. Admission is free for children up to 11 years old (€2 for adults) but it is totally worth it if we are interested in the whole story.
Hermitage of San Gaudioso. Circus of San Gaudioso. Moncayo Natural Park.
